title: gh-132470: Prevent crash in ctypes.CField when `byte_size` is incorrect
author: dura0ok
body:
When creating a ctypes.CField with an incorrect byte_size (e.g., using byte_size=2 for ctypes.c_byte), the code would previously abort due to the failed assertion byte_size == info->size.

This commit replaces the assertion with a proper error handling mechanism that raises a ValueError when byte_size does not match the expected type size. This prevents the crash and provides a more informative error message to the us




<!-- gh-issue-number: gh-132470 -->
* Issue: gh-132470
<!-- /gh-issue-number -->

comments.author: encukou
comments.body:
Thanks for getting this in everyone!


Python 3.13 does not allow creating `CField` from Python at all, and doesn't have a `byte_size` argument, so this shouldn't be backported.

@ZeroIntensity: A friendly bicker, just so you know my opinion: This should not have a NEWS entry, since the CField constructor is not public. If you call it (with `_internal_use=True`), you are using fragile undocumented internal API.
But, now that the entry it's there I don't think it's worth it to remove it.

comments.author: ZeroIntensity
comments.body: FWIW, I do sometimes see changelog entries for changes to the private API if people were relying on it. I don't know if that applied here.
